vendors.name: Oracle Aconex
vendors.tagline: Document control & project collaboration for mega-projects
vendors.market_position: The common-data-environment standard on Saudi giga-projects — Red Sea Global runs Aconex alongside Primavera across 23,000+ users. It handles the document control, RFIs, transmittals and multi-party collaboration that NEOM-scale work demands.
pros: Proven on the largest Saudi giga-projects (Red Sea Global and others)
Deep document control, RFI and submittal workflow
Neutral, fully auditable record across all project parties
cons: Not an accounting / ZATCA e-invoicing tool
vendors.sa_specific_note: A collaboration backbone for main contractors and project-management consultants on large projects, not a back-office system for small firms. ZATCA e-invoicing is handled separately by your accounting/ERP.

vendors.name: Oracle Primavera P6
vendors.tagline: Critical-path scheduling & cost control for large projects
vendors.market_position: The de-facto scheduling and portfolio-management tool on major Saudi projects, usually paired with Aconex. Built for the multi-thousand-activity programmes that giga-project main contractors and PMCs run.
pros: Widely required by giga-project clients and PMCs
Deep cost and resource management (with Unifier)
cons: Steep learning curve — needs a trained planner
vendors.sa_specific_note: If you bid on government or giga-project work, clients frequently mandate Primavera-format programmes — it is often a contractual requirement, not a choice.

vendors.name: Autodesk Construction Cloud
vendors.tagline: BIM, design collaboration & construction management
vendors.market_position: The BIM backbone on NEOM, Red Sea and Qiddiya. Combines design collaboration (BIM 360 / Forma), field management and document control — the standard where giga-project clients mandate BIM-to-fabrication workflows.
pros: Used across the flagship Saudi giga-projects
Required where clients mandate BIM (e.g. ROSHN)
cons: BIM depth is wasted on simple fit-out/MEP jobs
vendors.sa_specific_note: Giga-project clients increasingly require BIM-to-fabrication integration (ROSHN cites ~15% rework reduction). If you sub-contract into those projects, expect to work inside Autodesk's environment.

vendors.name: Procore
vendors.tagline: All-in-one construction project management
vendors.market_position: The global construction-management leader, with a regional office serving the UAE/Gulf. Strong for field, financials and document management on mid-to-large commercial builds — but lighter on Saudi-specific localisation than the local ERPs.
cons: No public pricing — quote-only (reported AED 80k+/yr enterprise, third-party estimate)
No native Arabic UI or ZATCA e-invoicing shown
Priced for established firms, not small contractors
vendors.typical_pricing: Quote-only; third parties report ~AED 80k+/yr at enterprise tier (unconfirmed by Procore).
vendors.sa_specific_note: Procore is excellent project software but isn't built around Saudi compliance — you'll still need a ZATCA-certified accounting tool (Qoyod, Wafeq, First Bit) alongside it for e-invoicing and VAT.

vendors.name: PlanRadar
vendors.tagline: Site inspections, snagging & defect management
vendors.market_position: The most accessible mid-market site-execution tool with a genuine Saudi presence and full Arabic site. Handles snagging, inspections, defect tracking and handover documentation — reachable for SMB contractors where Aconex and Procore are not.
pros: Affordable, per-user model with a 30-day trial
Fast to deploy — usable on day one
Strong for snagging, inspections and handover
cons: Focused on site execution, not full project/financial management
Pricing not published on the page (per-user, quote)
Less suited to heavy scheduling/BIM needs
vendors.typical_pricing: Per-user subscription (quote / 30-day free trial); no public SAR price.
vendors.sa_specific_note: The strongest entry point for small and mid-size Saudi contractors who need digital snagging and inspections without an enterprise rollout — and it works in Arabic out of the box.

vendors.name: Bentley Systems
vendors.tagline: Infrastructure BIM & 4D construction modelling
vendors.market_position: Infrastructure-grade BIM (OpenBuildings, SYNCHRO 4D) used on Saudi giga-projects for the asset-lifecycle and 4D-scheduling end of the work — strongest where the project is heavy civil/infrastructure rather than vertical building.
pros: Used on large Saudi infrastructure programmes
cons: Not for small contractors or back-office
vendors.sa_specific_note: Most relevant if you work on infrastructure, utilities or large civil packages within the giga-projects rather than standard building fit-out.

vendors.name: First Bit ERP
vendors.tagline: Construction-specific ERP: BOQ, estimating & project costing
vendors.market_position: The strongest Saudi-native construction ERP — genuine BOQ, estimating, project costing, procurement and payroll, with 2,000+ Middle East clients. Built around how Gulf contracting firms actually run, with ZATCA, withholding tax and Arabic baked in.
pros: Arabic and English; KSA + UAE offices and support
cons: ERP implementation takes time and setup
Heavier than a simple accounting app
Best value for established contracting firms
vendors.sa_specific_note: The best "one local differentiator" pick — a construction ERP that already speaks Saudi compliance (ZATCA, WHT) and Arabic, rather than a US tool bolted onto a separate invoicing system.

vendors.name: PACT ERP
vendors.tagline: Contracting ERP: planning, cost control & procurement
vendors.market_position: A Gulf-native contracting ERP (Riyadh HQ, with UAE/Bahrain/Qatar reach) covering project planning, cost control, procurement and payroll for contracting companies. Arabic-capable and tuned to the regional contracting workflow.
pros: Project cost control and procurement built in
cons: ZATCA support not explicitly stated — confirm before buying
Smaller ecosystem than the global tools
vendors.sa_specific_note: Confirm ZATCA Phase-2 e-invoicing support directly during the demo — it's standard for the category but not loudly published on the site.

vendors.name: Medad ERP
vendors.tagline: Saudi SME/contracting ERP: finance, HR & inventory
vendors.market_position: A Riyadh-based SME and contracting ERP covering finance, HR, inventory and sales, with ZATCA Phase-2 approval. A practical all-in-one for small-to-mid Saudi contracting and trade businesses that want local compliance without enterprise weight.
pros: Covers finance + HR + inventory in one
Aimed at SME budgets and complexity
vendors.sa_specific_note: A solid Saudi-native option for smaller contracting firms whose first priority is ZATCA-compliant finance + HR rather than heavy project controls.

vendors.name: Onyx Pro ERP
vendors.tagline: Long-established ERP widely used by Saudi contractors
vendors.market_position: A long-running Arabic ERP with deep adoption among Saudi contractors and SMEs — finance, projects and inventory with ZATCA support. Familiar and widely supported locally, if less modern than the newer cloud tools.
cons: Older interface vs newer cloud ERPs
vendors.sa_specific_note: Often shortlisted for its large local install base and support availability — worth comparing against newer cloud ERPs (First Bit, Medad) on usability before deciding.

vendors.name: Qoyod
vendors.tagline: Cloud accounting & ZATCA e-invoicing for contractors
vendors.market_position: A Saudi-native (Riyadh, 2016) cloud accounting platform, ZATCA Phase-2 certified, with one of the few publicly published price lists in the market. Strong for project firms and O&M contractors that need clean, compliant books without an ERP.
pros: Published, affordable SAR pricing (rare in this market)
Fast to set up for SMB contractors
cons: Trades firms may still need a separate PM tool
vendors.sa_specific_note: One of only two vendors here that publish SAR pricing. Pairs well with a site tool like PlanRadar: Qoyod for compliant books + e-invoicing, PlanRadar for site execution.

vendors.name: Wafeq
vendors.tagline: Modern accounting with per-project P&L & cost centres
vendors.market_position: A Saudi/Gulf-native accounting platform (KSA + UAE), ZATCA-compliant, with per-project profit-and-loss and cost-centre tracking that suits contractors. Modern UI, bilingual, and one of the few with published starting pricing.
pros: Per-project P&L and cost centres — useful for contractors
cons: Project-costing depth is lighter than a full ERP
vendors.typical_pricing: Published: from SAR 119/month (Starter); free tier available.
vendors.sa_specific_note: The per-project P&L is the standout for trades/contracting use — it gives small firms job-level profitability without a heavy ERP, while staying ZATCA-compliant.

vendors.name: Daftra
vendors.tagline: Arabic-first contractor management & accounting
vendors.market_position: An Arabic-first MENA platform with a dedicated contractor-management module — project tracking, statements, materials and labour costing — plus ZATCA support. Strong for Arabic-language SMEs that want one tool for jobs and books.
pros: Arabic-first, built for the MENA SME
Combines jobs, materials, labour and accounting
cons: Less construction-specialised than a true construction ERP
Breadth can mean shallower depth per module
vendors.typical_pricing: Tiered SaaS — pricing not publicly fixed (quote/sign-up).
vendors.sa_specific_note: A good fit for smaller Arabic-language contracting firms that want a single affordable system covering both project tracking and ZATCA-compliant accounting.

vendors.name: FSM Grid
vendors.tagline: AI field-service management for MEP & maintenance
vendors.market_position: A dedicated field-service platform — dispatch, work orders, IoT-driven predictive maintenance — with Arabic among its supported languages and regional operations. The standout where most Saudi FSM is generic Zoho/Dynamics rather than purpose-built.
pros: Strong fit for MEP, HVAC and facilities contractors
cons: Less name-recognition than the big ERPs
Best value once you have a real field workforce
vendors.sa_specific_note: If your business is service and maintenance (MEP, HVAC, FM) rather than building, this is more fit-for-purpose than a construction ERP — pair it with a ZATCA accounting tool for invoicing.

vendors.name: Focus Softnet (Centra CAFM)
vendors.tagline: CAFM + field-service ERP for facilities & MEP firms
vendors.market_position: A regional ERP house (Dubai-HQ, strong KSA presence) whose Centra CAFM covers computer-aided facilities management and field service for FM and MEP-service companies, with Arabic and ZATCA-ready accounting in the wider suite.
pros: CAFM + field service in one regional suite
Ties into ZATCA-ready Focus accounting modules
cons: Broad suite — can be more than a small firm needs
Less specialised than a pure FSM tool
vendors.sa_specific_note: Worth shortlisting for facilities-management and MEP-maintenance firms that want CAFM and accounting from one regional vendor with local support.

vendors.name: Saudi Council of Engineers (SCE)
vendors.tagline: الهيئة السعودية للمهندسين — engineering profession regulator
vendors.market_position: The regulator of the engineering profession in Saudi Arabia. SCE registration and accreditation is mandatory to legally practise engineering — and is tied to engineers' Iqama (residence permit) renewal.
pros: Mandatory for legal engineering practice in KSA
Registration is tied to professional accreditation
Required for many tendering and licensing steps
cons: Non-registration blocks legal practice and Iqama renewal
Requirements differ by grade and discipline
vendors.sa_specific_note: If your firm employs engineers, their SCE status directly affects your ability to operate and renew their residence — keep it current.

vendors.name: Contractor Classification (MOMRAH)
vendors.tagline: تصنيف المقاولين — grades contractors for public work
vendors.market_position: The national contractor-classification system run by the Ministry of Municipalities & Housing. It grades contractors by financial, technical and administrative capacity (Special category + Grades 1–6). No government body may award a project to an unclassified contractor.
pros: Required to bid on government/public projects
Grade signals capacity to clients (Grade 1 highest)
cons: Unclassified = locked out of public tenders
Higher grades require proven financials and track record
vendors.sa_specific_note: Your classification grade caps the project value you can bid on for public work — software that tracks financials and project history cleanly helps when you apply to move up a grade.

vendors.name: Balady Platform
vendors.tagline: بلدي — national municipal e-permitting
vendors.market_position: The national municipal e-services portal for building permits and related approvals — fully online. Construction work requires Balady permits, and the platform handles permit issuance, inquiry and modification.
pros: Single online portal for building permits
Integrates with the wider municipal services
cons: Permit submissions must meet Saudi Building Code
Process is fully digital — paperwork must be in order
vendors.sa_specific_note: Permit-ready documentation (SBC-compliant drawings, classifications) speeds Balady approvals — keeping project documents organised in your PM/ERP tool pays off here.

vendors.name: Saudi Building Code (SBC)
vendors.tagline: كود البناء السعودي — the mandatory national building code
vendors.market_position: The national building code, legally mandatory under Royal Decree. Compliance with SBC (structural, mechanical/HVAC, electrical, plumbing, energy and fire codes) is required to obtain building permits.
pros: The single national standard for building compliance
Covers structural, MEP, energy and fire safety
vendors.sa_specific_note: SBC replaces US codes (IBC/IRC/NEC) — drawings, MEP specs and permit submissions must conform to SBC, so design and QA tools need Saudi-code templates, not US ones.

vendors.name: ZATCA E-Invoicing (Fatoora)
vendors.tagline: فاتورة — mandatory e-invoicing & tax integration
vendors.market_position: The Zakat, Tax & Customs Authority runs Saudi Arabia's mandatory e-invoicing system (Fatoora). Phase 2 requires API integration, real-time clearance, UUID, cryptographic stamp and QR on invoices — for all VAT-registered businesses, rolled out in waves.
pros: Defines what compliant invoicing software must do
QR + cryptographic stamp standardise B2B invoices
vendors.typical_pricing: Government mandate (no fee; software must be compliant).
vendors.sa_specific_note: This is the single biggest reason US/Western accounting tools don't work in KSA. Choose a ZATCA Phase-2-certified system — Qoyod, Wafeq, First Bit, Medad, Daftra all qualify.

vendors.name: Qiwa & Nitaqat (Saudization)
vendors.tagline: قوى / نطاقات — labour & Saudization compliance
vendors.market_position: Qiwa is the digital labour portal (contracts, work permits) and Nitaqat is the Saudization quota system it calculates. A firm's Nitaqat band (Platinum/Green/Red) controls its ability to get visas, renew Iqamas and bid on government work.
pros: Centralises employment contracts and work permits
Nitaqat band affects tenders and visas
cons: A low/Red band blocks visas, renewals and tenders
Construction quotas are demanding to hit
vendors.sa_specific_note: Nitaqat has no US equivalent — HR/payroll software used in KSA must track nationality ratios and Qiwa-documented contracts, not just hours and pay.

vendors.name: Saudi Contractors Authority (Muqawil)
vendors.tagline: الهيئة السعودية للمقاولين — contracting-sector authority
vendors.market_position: The authority that develops and regulates the contracting sector (public-facing portal "Muqawil"). Registration is required by the Ministry of Finance to receive government projects, and the body provides sector support and development programmes.
pros: Registration needed to receive government projects
vendors.sa_specific_note: Together with MOMRAH classification and Etimad registration, SCA membership is part of the "can I legally win public work?" checklist for Saudi contractors.

vendors.name: Etimad Platform
vendors.tagline: اعتماد — government tenders, procurement & payments
vendors.market_position: The Ministry of Finance's unified portal for government tenders, procurement and payments. Suppliers and contractors must be registered on Etimad to bid on, contract for, or get paid on public projects.
pros: Single portal for all government tenders & payments
Required to bid on and get paid for public work
vendors.sa_specific_note: Etimad is where Saudi public-sector work is actually won and paid — clean, classification-ready financials (from your ERP/accounting tool) make registration and bidding far smoother.
